hallucinations.description: Multiple references in Section 2 and the References list cite papers from 2025 (or with future arXiv IDs) that do not exist, including Tayebati et al. (2025), Zhou and Cheng (2025), Xu (2025), Gan et al. (2025), and the survey 'Explainable AI for Large Language Models: A Survey' (2025). These appear fabricated to support the related-work narrative.
hallucinations.evidence: References 3-7, 16: 'Tayebati et al. (2025). arXiv:2502.06884', 'Zhou and Cheng (2025). arXiv:2510.22931', 'Xu (2025). arXiv:2509.00461', 'Gan et al. (2025). arXiv:2510.26026', 'Explainable AI for Large Language Models: A Survey. (2025). arXiv:2501.04567'.

overall_assessment: The paper contains hallucinations in the form of nonexistent citations. The methodology, equations, experimental setup, and reported metrics are consistent with the provided source code and appear non-fabricated. No mathematical errors or faked results were identified.
